title: Math — Linear Equations (Two Variables)
description: SAT-style two-variable linear equation questions: interpreting coefficients, slope/intercepts, linear models, and graph transformations.

questions.prompt: The equation \(5G+45R=380\) represents the total points a student earned by collecting \(G\) green tokens and \(R\) red tokens. How many more points is a red token worth than a green token?
questions.explanation: The coefficients represent points per token: green is 5 points, red is 45 points. Difference: \(45-5=40\).

questions.prompt: In the xy-plane, line \(k\) passes through \((0,-5)\) and \((1,-1)\). Which equation defines line \(k\)?
questions.explanation: Slope \(m=\frac{-1-(-5)}{1-0}=\frac{4}{1}=4\). Since the line passes through \((0,-5)\), the y-intercept is −5. So \(y=4x-5\).

questions.prompt: In the xy-plane, line \(p\) has slope \(-\frac{5}{3}\) and x-intercept \((-6,0)\). What is the y-coordinate of the y-intercept of line \(p\)?

Figures A and B are regular polygons. The sum of their perimeters is represented by \(3a+6b=63\), where \(a\) and \(b\) are the numbers of sides of figures A and B, respectively. Which statement best interprets the meaning of 6 in this context?
choices: Each side of figure B has a length of 6 inches.
The number of sides of figure B is 6.
Each side of figure A has a length of 6 inches.
The number of sides of figure A is 6.
questions.explanation: Perimeter = (side length) × (number of sides). Since 6 multiplies \(b\) (the number of sides of figure B), 6 represents the side length of figure B (in inches).

questions.prompt: A neighborhood has a 2-hectare park and a 35-hectare residential area with a total of 3,934 trees. The equation \(2x+35y=3934\) represents this situation. What does \(x\) represent?
choices: The average number of trees per hectare in the park
The average number of trees per hectare in the residential area
The total number of trees in the park
The total number of trees in the residential area
questions.explanation: The coefficient 2 is the number of park hectares, so \(2x\) is (hectares)×(trees per hectare). Thus \(x\) must be the average trees per hectare in the park.

questions.prompt: In the xy-plane, line \(t\) passes through \((0,9)\) and \((1,17)\). Which equation defines line \(t\)?
questions.explanation: Slope \(m=\frac{17-9}{1-0}=8\). Passing through \((0,9)\) gives y-intercept 9, so \(y=8x+9\).

questions.prompt: An artist paints and sells square tiles. The selling price \(P\) (dollars) is a linear function of side length \(s\) (inches). The table shows that when \(s\) increases by 3, \(P\) increases by 10. Which equation defines the relationship?
questions.explanation: Rate of change \(m=\frac{10}{3}\) dollars per inch. Using a point from the table (implied by the problem’s original rationale) gives intercept −2, so \(P=\frac{10}{3}s-2\).

questions.prompt: If the graph of \(27x+33y=297\) is shifted down 5 units in the xy-plane, what is the y-intercept of the resulting graph?
questions.explanation: Original y-intercept: set \(x=0\): \(33y=297\Rightarrow y=9\), so \((0,9)\). Shifting down 5 makes the y-value 5 less: \((0,4)\).

questions.prompt: What is the slope of the graph of \(y=\frac{1}{3}(29x+10)+5x\)?

A city is planning to build a rock retaining wall and a garden. Limestone weighs 120 lb/ft\(^3\) and basalt weighs 180 lb/ft\(^3\). If 330 pounds of granite will also be used and the total weight of all rocks is 1,000 pounds, which equation represents this situation?
questions.explanation: Granite contributes 330 lb, leaving \(1000-330=670\) lb for limestone and basalt. Limestone weight is \(120x\), basalt weight is \(180y\). So \(120x+180y=670\).
